sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Chelsea are in strong form, having won 4 of their last 5 matches, including impressive victories against Napoli (3-2) and Crystal Palace (3-1). West Ham United have shown some resilience with 3 wins in their last 5, including a 2-1 win over Tottenham, but they remain inconsistent, with a recent 3-0 loss to Wolverhampton. Chelsea's attacking prowess and home advantage at Stamford Bridge give them the edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Chelsea have dominated West Ham, securing a 5-1 away victory and a 2-1 home win. The head-to-head stats over the last 5 matchups show Chelsea with 4 wins to West Ham's 1, with matches often producing over 2.5 goals (5/5) and high corner counts (6/7 over 10.5), indicating an open, attacking game is likely.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Chelsea’s João Pedro has been in fine form, scoring 4 goals in the last 5 matches with a Sofascore rating of 7.46, while Cole Palmer remains a threat with 2 goals and 2 assists recently. For West Ham, Crysencio Summerville stands out with 3 goals and a rating of 7.7 in the last 5 games, posing a potential counter-attacking danger.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Chelsea, sitting 5th with 37 points, boast a strong attack (39 goals, 4th in the league) and solid defense (25 conceded, 3rd least), with high possession (57%) and corner averages (5.7 per game). West Ham, languishing in 18th with 20 points, have struggled defensively (45 goals conceded), and their away form remains a concern with no clean sheets in 21 matches.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Chelsea’s superior form, home advantage, and historical dominance over West Ham, a home win seems likely, aligning with Sofascore's 65% probability. Expect an attacking game with over 2.5 goals, as seen in recent head-to-heads, though West Ham’s recent scoring form suggests they might find the net. A physical match with multiple corners and cards is also anticipated due to both teams’ foul counts and the referee’s average (3.8 yellows per game).

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, West Ham United lead Chelsea 2-0 at Stamford Bridge. The away side struck early with a goal in the 7th minute and added a second in the 36th minute, showcasing clinical finishing. Chelsea, despite dominating possession, have struggled to convert their control into meaningful chances, trailing significantly at the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Chelsea have controlled the game with a significant share of possession and numerous entries into the final third and penalty area. However, their attacking output has been underwhelming, with only a handful of shots on target and low expected goals. Defensively, they've been vulnerable, conceding twice from West Ham's more efficient attacks. West Ham, on the other hand, have made the most of their opportunities, converting a decent expected goals figure into two actual goals. Their defensive structure has held firm despite less possession, limiting Chelsea's threat in key areas.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Chelsea started the game with strong attacking intent, pushing forward with consistent pressure in the early stages. They maintained this momentum for much of the first half, with several spells of dominance in the opposition half. However, West Ham showed sharpness in key moments, particularly around their goals, where they shifted the momentum briefly but effectively. Towards the end of the half, Chelsea's attacking surges waned slightly, while West Ham capitalized with dangerous bursts forward, especially in the latter minutes.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and contributions
halftimeSections.text: For West Ham, Jarrod Bowen and Crysencio Summerville have been pivotal, each finding the net and posing a constant threat. Aaron Wan-Bissaka also stood out with two assists, underlining his importance in their attacking setup. On Chelsea's side, players like Cole Palmer and Enzo Fernández have shown glimpses of creativity, but the team lacks the final touch in front of goal. Defensively, Benoit Badiashile has been solid with clearances, but the overall backline has struggled to contain West Ham's sharp attacks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Chelsea are likely to come out with renewed urgency in the second half, potentially introducing fresh legs to boost their attacking threat. They need to improve their finishing and exploit their dominance in possession to break down West Ham's defense. West Ham, meanwhile, may adopt a more defensive posture to protect their lead, relying on counter-attacks to catch Chelsea off guard. Their ability to maintain composure under pressure will be key to holding or extending their advantage.
